chunks.text: We look up at the night sky.
At that moment, what we see is light that has traveled for thousands of years.
In 1609, Galileo Galilei looked at the sky through a telescope he had built himself.
There were mountains on the face of the moon.
Around Jupiter, there were four moons.
These observation shook the ancient belief that the earth stood at the center of the universe.
Four hundred years later, in 1977, NASA launched two probe.
They explored Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune in turn.
Even now, they continue their quiet journey beyond the solar system.
The sounds of the earth, human words, human music.
It is a small message from humanity to any being who might, someday
find it somewhere in the vast universe.
From Galileo's telescope to Voyager's journey, our curiosity continues beyond the millennium
toward the far end of the universe.
